objective: [Momentum] sur 0GUSDC en 1h. Indicateurs : EMA + AROON + MACD. Entrées : S&R, Fibonacci Levels; Sorties : SL/TP ou risk management. Utilise l'EMA pour suivre les tendances courantes et l'AROON pour identifier des zones de retour a la moyenne. Combine le MACD pour repérer les zones d'indicateurs convergents et divergentes qui peuvent indiquer une potentielle change de direction. Utilise un SL/TP approprié selon ces indicateurs pour limiter l'exposition au risque.

iterations.hypothesis: Combining momentum indicators EMA and Aroon with volatility-based risk management using ATR along with trend strength analysis via ADX, Bollinger bands, and identifying zones of converging/diverging momentum to identify potential trade entry points.

pre_reflection.text: [Pre-reflection] The strategy combines multiple momentum indicators, volatility-based risk management using ATR and trend strength analysis via ADX, Bollinger bands to identify potential trade entry points. The use of EMA, Aroon, and MACD along with the implementation of appropriate SL/TP can help in limiting exposure to risk while seeking opportunities for profit. However, the strategy may be sensitive to market conditions or unexpected changes that could affect its effectiveness. In case of poor returns (negative return), the specific change would involve revisiting the choice of EMA and Aroon thresholds as well as adjusting SL/TP levels based on real-time market data. For low Sharpe ratio, an increase in volatility bands can be considered to enhance trade opportunities while maintaining risk management. An alternative approach could be exploring other momentum indicators or incorporating fundamental analysis into the strategy.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] Iteration 3/10 - Momentum strategy using EMA, AROON and MACD with SL/TP. Focuses on following trends and identifying areas of convergence or divergence in the market data for potential trade opportunities. Uses RSI, EMA and atr as indicators for entries and exits. If results are poor (negative return), would consider changing SL/TP based on market conditions or re-evaluating entry signals.
[Backup plan] If strategy fails to perform well, could try using different trend following strategies that rely less heavily on momentum but still utilize the same technical analysis tools. Another approach could be to test a mean reversion strategy with different indicators and parameters, such as those used in relative strength index (RSI) or moving average convergence divergence (MACD). Additionally, if results are mediocre (low Sharpe), looking into adding more diversification through alternative strategies or assets may help improve performance.

iterations.hypothesis: Momentum signal using EMA and AROON with trend reversal filter through ADX.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The given strategy uses momentum indicators to identify potential trend reversals in AAVEUSDC 1h market. It takes into account Bollinger Bands, RSI and ATR for entries/exits and risk management. Weaknesses include the use of only one asset and potential overfitting due to high number of variables used. In case results are poor (negative return), we would consider reducing the number of indicators or using a more diversified set of assets. If Sharpe is low, we could look into optimizing parameters for better performance. Alternative approaches might involve considering multiple timeframes, incorporating different types of market data and exploring other machine learning techniques.
[Backup plan] 1) Reduce the number of variables used in the strategy to avoid overfitting. 2) Optimize parameter settings based on backtest results. 3) Incorporate more diversified assets for comparison with AAVEUSDC. 4) Explore alternative strategies, such as those involving multiple timeframes or incorporating different types of market data.
